capability_objective: Resolve one uniquely constrained record from a large distractor-heavy meeting register through an exact search, gate full-record retrieval on the stable ID returned by that search, avoid forbidden read and write side paths, and render the retrieved fields in an exact compact table.
prompt: I’m reconciling the meeting register. Use the genuine local meeting client at `./meetingctl`; its built-in help describes its executable interface. Locate the active meeting named “Quarterly Capacity Review” at “Denver”. Search with that exact name and location, require the search to resolve exactly one match, then use only the stable ID returned by that search to retrieve its full record. Same-name entries elsewhere, near-name entries at Denver, and similar IDs are distractors. This is read-only: do not list the register broadly, check availability, update or cancel anything, or send a notification. Write the result to `meeting-record.md` as a compact two-column Markdown table labeled `Field` and `Value`. Use the literal header row `| Field | Value |`, the literal separator row `|---|---|`, and the data-row pattern `| FIELD | VALUE |`. After the separator, include exactly these rows in this order: ID, name, location, status, and date. End the final date row with one newline. Do not put a heading, blank line, or any other prose in that file. Finish your response with the same table.
